description: Reference to the customer for whom this wafer lot is being fabricated. Applicable for foundry/OSAT business models where lots are customer-specific. Null for internal product lots. This is the PARTY_REFERENCE category field for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity.

Foreign key linking to product.compliance_cert. Business justification: Wafer lots may require compliance certifications (ITAR, REACH, RoHS) for export/shipment. Cert status tracking for trade compliance and regulatory reporting requires this link.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Required for allocating wafer‑lot production costs to a cost center for internal cost‑of‑goods‑sold reporting.

Foreign key linking to customer.customer_design_win. Business justification: Required for Production Tracking Report linking each wafer lot to the originating customer design win for revenue and yield attribution.

Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: ECCN classification is needed for export control of wafer lots; linking lot to its ECCN record enables automated compliance checks and reporting.

Foreign key linking to product.errata. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by errata (silicon bugs). Errata impact on WIP for disposition, customer notification, and workaround implementation requires this link.

Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Export License Management requires each wafer lot to be tied to the specific export license authorizing its shipment, a standard practice in semiconductor export compliance.

Reference to the specific equipment unit currently processing or last processing this wafer lot. Enables equipment-to-lot traceability for defect root cause analysis and equipment qualification tracking.
reference: Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; Camstar MES; SEMI E10

description: Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Wafer lots produce die for specific product families. Revenue forecasting, capacity allocation, and family-level WIP tracking require this link for business planning.

Foreign key linking to product.ic_catalog. Business justification: Required for Wafer Lot Production Traceability Report linking each wafer lot to its IC catalog; replaces denormalized product_code with a proper FK.
Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Wafer‑lot allocation to a specific IC design project is required for production planning and capacity reports.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Wafer lot ownership by legal entity required for transfer pricing, customs valuation, intercompany transactions, and consolidated financial reporting in multi-entity semiconductor operations.

Foreign key linking to product.product_ltb_notification. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by LTB notifications (product discontinuance). LTB impact on WIP for disposition, customer notification, and last-time-buy planning requires this link.

Reference to the MPW shuttle run if this lot is part of a multi-project wafer program. MPW lots share a single wafer with multiple customer designs to reduce NRE costs. Null for dedicated production lots.

Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Wafer lots may be affected by PCNs (process changes). PCN impact on WIP for disposition decisions, customer notification, and change management requires this link.

Identifier of the MES process route (flow plan) assigned to this wafer lot, defining the ordered sequence of operations from wafer start through final FAB step. Determines which process steps, equipment, and recipes apply to the lot.
reference: Camstar MES; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; SEMI E10

description: Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Wafer lots are fabricated on specific process nodes. Yield tracking, technology migration, capacity planning, and fab analytics require direct process node linkage.

Foreign key linking to product.product_qualification_program. Business justification: Wafer lots may be part of qualification programs (reliability testing, customer qual). Qual program participation tracking for certification and production release requires this link.

Foreign key linking to product.product_spec. Business justification: Wafer lots are fabricated to meet product specs. Yield analysis, process control, and spec compliance tracking for production release require product_spec linkage.
Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Enables profitability analysis per wafer lot by linking to the profit center that owns the product line.
Reference to the SAP SD sales order that triggered this wafer lot start. Links WIP inventory to customer demand for order-to-cash traceability and delivery commitment tracking.
FK to product.sku

Foreign key linking to inventory.storage_location. Business justification: WIP wafer lots are physically stored in specific cleanroom storage locations within the FAB. The existing storage_location_code (STRING) is a denormalized reference that should be replaced by a proper

Reference to the tapeout record (final design submission for manufacturing) associated with this wafer lot. Links WIP inventory to the design domain for design-to-silicon traceability and first silicon yield analysis.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM; Oracle Agile PLM; design.tapeout

description: Reference to the wafer start authorization record that approved and initiated this lot. Provides traceability from WIP inventory back to the demand signal and capacity authorization event.

Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Wafer lots produced for customer projects or NRE work track to WBS elements for project costing, margin analysis, and milestone billing.

Reference to the SAP work center or MES equipment group where the lot is currently queued or being processed. Enables WIP queue analysis and bottleneck identification across FEOL, MOL, and BEOL process stages.
Actual date on which the wafer lot completed all FAB process steps. Populated upon lot completion. Used for cycle time actuals, on-time delivery KPI calculation, and process improvement analysis.

Timestamp when this wafer lot master record was first created in the inventory data product (Silver layer). Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_CREATED category for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for data lineage and audit trail.

The current process operation step name or code at which the wafer lot is positioned in the process flow (e.g., 'FEOL_Gate_Oxide', 'MOL_Contact_Etch', 'BEOL_Metal1_CVD'). Sourced from the MES process flow definition. Critical for WIP traceability and cycle time analysis.
reference: Camstar MES; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; SEMI E10

description: Theoretical number of die that can be patterned on a single wafer for this product at the given process node and die size. Used as the denominator for yield calculations and finished goods quantity forecasting.
reference: SEMI; Camstar MES; KLA ICOS Inspection Systems

description: Number of wafers in the lot that have passed all in-line inspection and metrology checks to date. Represents the current count of wafers expected to yield good die. Updated after each inspection or disposition event.
reference: KLA ICOS Inspection Systems; Camstar MES; SEMI E10

description: Indicates whether the wafer lot is currently placed on hold and cannot proceed to the next process step. A True value means the lot is blocked pending engineering review, quality disposition, or management approval. Sourced from MES hold management.
reference: Camstar MES; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; ISO 9001

description: Coded reason for the current or most recent hold placed on the wafer lot (e.g., 'PROCESS_EXCURSION', 'EQUIPMENT_FAILURE', 'YIELD_LOSS', 'CUSTOMER_REQUEST', 'ENGINEERING_REVIEW'). Populated when hold_flag is True. Used for hold disposition tracking and FMEA analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: PROCESS_EXCURSION|EQUIPMENT_FAILURE|YIELD_LOSS|CUSTOMER_REQUEST|ENGINEERING_REVIEW|MATERIAL_SHORTAGE|QUALITY_DISPOSITION — promote to reference product]
reference: Camstar MES; SAP S/4HANA QM; SEMI E10; ISO 9001; IATF 16949

description: Date and time when the most recent hold was placed on the wafer lot. Null if the lot has never been placed on hold. Used for hold duration tracking and SPC (Statistical Process Control) excursion response time measurement.
reference: Camstar MES; SAP S/4HANA QM; SEMI E10

description: Current standard cost valuation of the wafer lot in the inventory ledger, expressed in the functional currency. Calculated based on the number of wafers and the standard cost per wafer at the current process step. Used for WIP inventory valuation in SAP FI/CO and financial reporting under SOX.

Type of photolithography exposure technology used for the critical layers of this wafer lot. EUV (Extreme Ultraviolet) is used for leading-edge nodes (sub-7nm); DUV (Deep Ultraviolet) for mature nodes. Determines equipment routing and cost structure.
reference: SEMI; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; IEEE

description: Calendar date on which the wafer lot was officially started (wafer start authorization executed) in the FAB. Used for cycle time measurement, WIP aging analysis, and on-time delivery tracking. This is the BUSINESS_EVENT_TIMESTAMP (date precision) category field.
reference: Camstar MES; SAP S/4HANA PP; SEMI E10

description: Current lifecycle state of the wafer lot within the FAB WIP flow. 'active' indicates the lot is progressing through process steps; 'on_hold' indicates a hold flag is set pending disposition; 'completed' indicates all process steps are finished; 'scrapped' indicates the lot has been disposed; 'cancelled' indicates the lot was terminated before completion. This is the LIFECYCLE_STATUS category field.
reference: Camstar MES; SAP S/4HANA PP; SEMI E10

description: Classification of the wafer lot by its manufacturing purpose. 'production' lots are for customer shipment; 'engineering' lots support process development; 'qualification' lots validate new processes or products; 'monitor' lots track process stability; 'reliability' lots support JEDEC/IATF reliability testing. This is the CLASSIFICATION_OR_TYPE category field.

Native lot reference number as recorded in the Applied Materials SmartFactory or Camstar MES system. Enables direct cross-reference to the operational system of record for lot tracking, dispatch, and WIP management.
reference: Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; Camstar MES

description: Dispatch priority classification assigned to the wafer lot, used by MES to sequence lot movement through FAB equipment queues. 'hot' lots receive highest priority for customer-critical or yield-recovery situations; 'expedite' for accelerated delivery; 'standard' for normal flow; 'low' for non-urgent engineering runs.
reference: Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; Camstar MES; SEMI E3

business_glossary_term: Process Stage (Front End of Line / Middle of Line / Back End of Line)
description: High-level semiconductor fabrication stage classification: FEOL (Front End of Line) covers transistor formation; MOL (Middle of Line) covers local interconnects; BEOL (Back End of Line) covers metal interconnect layers. Used for WIP stage-gate reporting and cycle time decomposition.
reference: SEMI; IEEE; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Version identifier of the process route assigned to this lot. Tracks which revision of the process flow was active at lot start, supporting process change impact analysis and PCN (Product Change Notification) traceability.
reference: Camstar MES; Oracle Agile PLM; SEMI E10

description: Cumulative number of wafers scrapped from this lot since lot creation. Includes wafers scrapped due to process excursions, equipment failures, or quality dispositions. Used for yield loss analysis and DPPM (Defective Parts Per Million) reporting.
reference: Camstar MES; SAP S/4HANA QM; SEMI E10; ISO 9001

description: Code identifying the operational system of record from which this wafer lot record was sourced (e.g., CAMSTAR for Camstar MES, SMARTFACTORY for Applied Materials SmartFactory, SAP_PP for SAP Production Planning). Supports data lineage and multi-system reconciliation in the Databricks Silver layer.
reference: Camstar MES; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Planned date by which the wafer lot is expected to complete all FAB process steps and be ready for wafer probe/test. Derived from the process flow cycle time and lot start date. Used for delivery commitment and capacity planning.

Timestamp when this wafer lot master record was last modified in the inventory data product. Represents the RECORD_AUDIT_UPDATED category for this TRANSACTION_HEADER entity. Used for incremental data pipeline processing and audit trail.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the inventory valuation amount (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SOX compliance.
Current number of wafers remaining in the lot after accounting for any scrapped or split wafers during processing. Reflects real-time WIP inventory quantity for this lot.
reference: Camstar MES; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Number of wafers in the lot at the time of lot creation (wafer start). Typically 25 wafers per FOUP/cassette for standard lots. Used as the baseline denominator for yield and scrap rate calculations. This is the QUANTITATIVE_RESULT / principal measured quantity for this WIP transaction.
reference: Camstar MES; SEMI E47; SAP S/4HANA PP

description: Physical diameter of the silicon wafers in this lot, in millimeters. Standard values are 200mm (8-inch) and 300mm (12-inch). Determines equipment compatibility, cassette type, and cost per wafer.
Primary key for photomask_asset

Foreign key linking to product.configuration_rule. Business justification: Photomasks may support specific configurations (mask options). Configuration tracking for mask reuse, NRE allocation, and variant support requires this link.

Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license. Business justification: Photomasks embody controlled technology requiring export licenses for cross-border transfers to offshore fabs, OSAT partners, or customer sites. Critical for EAR/ITAR compliance in advanced node semic

Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_technology_node. Business justification: photomask_asset.technology_node is a denormalized text field. A proper FK to fabrication_technology_node supports mask qualification status per node, export control classification, and retirement deci

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Photomask sets are designed for product families, not individual ICs. NRE amortization, mask reuse analysis, and family-level mask inventory planning require this link.

Foreign key linking to finance.fixed_asset. Business justification: Photomasks are capitalized assets requiring linkage to fixed asset register for depreciation tracking, asset valuation, and financial reporting compliance.
Reference to the IC design project for which this photomask was created. Links the mask asset to the product design domain for end-to-end traceability from design intent to physical mask.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM, Oracle Agile PLM

description: Foreign key linking to product.product_ltb_notification. Business justification: Photomasks may be affected by LTB notifications (product discontinuance). LTB impact on mask inventory for retirement planning and asset disposal requires this link.

Foreign key linking to fabrication.mask_set. Business justification: photomask_asset is the physical inventory record of a reticle; mask_set is the engineering definition. Linking them enables mask set utilization tracking, reticle lifecycle management, and fab schedul

Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Photomasks may be affected by PCNs (reticle changes, OPC updates). PCN impact on mask inventory for requalification and production release requires this link.
Reference to the Process Design Kit (PDK) version under which this mask was designed and qualified. Ensures mask-process compatibility and supports PDK version change impact analysis.
Externally-known unique identifier assigned to the photomask by the mask vendor or internal mask management system. Used for cross-system traceability and vendor communication.

Foreign key linking to design.physical_layout. Business justification: Photomasks are manufactured from specific physical layout GDS files. Essential for mask-to-design traceability, reticle qualification against layout DRC/LVS results, defect dispositioning, and OPC/RET
FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — High-value photomasks must track their storage location for retrieval and environmental control.

Foreign key linking to fabrication.fabrication_process_flow. Business justification: Photomask assets are qualified and allocated to specific process flows for reticle scheduling and mask utilization reporting. Fab reticle management systems track which process flow each mask asset su

Foreign key linking to product.product_qualification_program. Business justification: Photomasks may require qualification (reticle qual, OPC verification). Qual program status tracking for production release and mask certification requires this link.
Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PHOTOMASK PROCUREMENT: Mask vendor is a supplier; linking enables vendor performance, cost, and compliance reporting.
Reference to the tapeout record that generated the GDS/GDSII data used to manufacture this photomask. Links the mask asset to the design domain for full design-to-fab traceability.
reference: Siemens Teamcenter PLM, Oracle Agile PLM

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.technology_control_plan. Business justification: Photomasks contain controlled design IP requiring technology control plans for deemed export compliance, foreign national access restrictions, clean team protocols, and physical/IT security measures i

Financial book value of the photomask asset in US dollars as recorded in the fixed asset or inventory valuation module. EUV masks can cost $500K–$1M+; DUV masks range from $10K–$100K. Used for inventory valuation, depreciation, and insurance purposes.

Measured critical dimension (CD) uniformity across the mask in nanometers, as determined during mask inspection. A key quality metric indicating pattern fidelity; tighter CD uniformity is required at advanced nodes.
reference: SEMI P1, KLA ICOS Inspection Systems, ITRS/IRDS

description: Timestamp when this photomask asset record was first created in the inventory management system. Provides audit trail for record provenance.

Total number of defects detected on the photomask surface during the most recent inspection cycle. Compared against the maximum allowable defect specification to determine pass/fail disposition. Tracked per KLA ICOS inspection records.
reference: KLA ICOS Inspection Systems, SEMI P1

description: Code identifying the fabrication facility (FAB) where this photomask is assigned for use. A mask may be qualified for use at a specific fab site due to equipment compatibility and process qualification requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Result of the most recent mask inspection performed by metrology equipment (e.g., KLA ICOS). Indicates whether the mask meets defect density and CD uniformity specifications for continued production use.
reference: KLA ICOS Inspection Systems, SEMI P1, ISO 9001

description: Indicates whether this photomask was manufactured as part of a Multi-Project Wafer (MPW) shuttle run, where multiple designs share a single mask set to reduce NRE costs. MPW masks have shared ownership and usage constraints.
Date on which the most recent mask inspection was performed using metrology equipment (e.g., KLA ICOS). Used to schedule next inspection interval and assess mask health currency.
reference: KLA ICOS Inspection Systems, SEMI P1

description: Timestamp of the most recent update to this photomask asset record. Used for change tracking, data lineage, and audit compliance.
Name of the photolithography process layer this mask patterns (e.g., POLY, METAL1, VIA2, DIFFUSION). Identifies the specific fabrication step in the FEOL/MOL/BEOL stack.

Classification of the lithography exposure technology for which this mask is designed. Extreme Ultraviolet (EUV) masks are used for sub-7nm nodes; Deep Ultraviolet (DUV) masks are used for 7nm and above. Drives storage, handling, and cost classification.
reference: SEMI P37 (EUV Mask), SEMI P1 (DUV Mask)

description: Date on which the photomask was manufactured and released by the mask vendor. Used for age-based shelf life tracking and vendor lead time analysis.

Version or generation identifier of the mask corresponding to the tapeout revision (e.g., v1.0, v2.1, ECO_rev3). Tracks which design tapeout iteration this mask was manufactured from, enabling traceability to the GDS/GDSII submission.
reference: Oracle Agile PLM, Siemens Teamcenter PLM

description: Physical size of the photomask substrate in millimeters (e.g., 152mm for standard 6-inch reticle). Determines compatibility with the lithography scanner chuck and handling equipment.

Current lifecycle status of the photomask asset. Active masks are available for production use; quarantined masks are held pending defect review; retired masks have exceeded usage limits or been superseded; scrapped masks have been physically destroyed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: active|quarantined|retired|in_inspection|pending_qualification|scrapped — promote to reference product]
reference: SEMI E10, Oracle Agile PLM, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Material of the photomask substrate. Fused silica and quartz are standard for DUV masks; Low Thermal Expansion Material (LTEM) and Ultra-Low Expansion (ULE) glass are used for EUV masks to minimize thermal distortion.

Physical mask type classification: binary (chrome-on-glass), attenuated phase-shifting mask (attPSM), alternating phase-shifting mask (altPSM), chromeless phase lithography (CPL), or EUV reflective mask. Determines optical proximity correction (OPC) strategy and imaging performance.
Specification-defined maximum number of defects permitted on the photomask before it must be quarantined or retired. Established during mask qualification per process node requirements.

Manufacturer- or process-qualified maximum number of exposure shots allowed before mandatory retirement or re-qualification of the photomask. Exceeding this limit triggers automatic quarantine in the MES.
reference: SEMI P1, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES, ISO 9001

description: Mask Error Enhancement Factor (MEEF) quantifying how mask CD (critical dimension) errors are amplified onto the wafer. A MEEF > 1 indicates that mask errors are magnified; used in process window analysis and mask specification setting.
Scheduled date by which the next mandatory mask inspection must be completed, based on usage count thresholds or calendar-based inspection intervals defined in the mask management plan.
reference: SEMI P1, ISO 9001, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Non-Recurring Engineering (NRE) cost in USD associated with the design and manufacture of this photomask set. NRE costs are one-time charges incurred at tapeout and are tracked for product cost accounting and customer billing.

Version identifier of the Optical Proximity Correction (OPC) recipe applied during mask data preparation. OPC compensates for diffraction effects in photolithography; version tracking ensures reproducibility and supports yield analysis.

Indicates whether a protective pellicle membrane is currently mounted on the photomask. Pellicles protect the mask pattern from particle contamination during exposure. EUV masks use specialized EUV-transparent pellicles.

Type of pellicle membrane mounted on the photomask. Standard pellicles are used for DUV masks; EUV pellicles are specialized thin-film membranes for EUV lithography. None indicates no pellicle is installed.

Measured overlay registration error of the mask pattern in nanometers, indicating how accurately the mask features are positioned relative to the design intent. Critical for multi-layer overlay budget management.
reference: SEMI P1, KLA ICOS Inspection Systems

description: Identifier of the Reticle Storage Pod (RSP) or reticle SMIF pod in which this photomask is currently housed. RSP tracking is critical for contamination prevention and automated mask library management.
reference: SEMI E84, SEMI E1.9, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Date on which the photomask was retired from production use, either due to exceeding usage limits, excessive defect count, design obsolescence, or end-of-life (EOL) of the associated product. Nullable for active masks.
reference: SEMI P1, Oracle Agile PLM, SAP S/4HANA MM

description: Model identifier of the lithography scanner (stepper/scanner) for which this photomask is qualified (e.g., ASML NXT:2000i, ASML NXE:3600D). Masks are qualified to specific scanner models due to optical system compatibility.
reference: SEMI P1, Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Required relative humidity percentage for photomask storage environment. Controlled humidity prevents electrostatic discharge damage and contamination on the mask substrate.
Required storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this photomask. EUV masks and phase-shifting masks may have tighter temperature requirements to prevent pellicle degradation or substrate stress.
Cumulative number of wafer exposure shots (uses) this photomask has undergone since manufacture or last qualification. Tracked by the MES to manage mask wear and schedule preventive inspection.
reference: Applied Materials SmartFactory MES, Camstar MES, SEMI E10

description: Unique system-generated surrogate key identifying a raw material master record in the semiconductor inventory domain. Primary key for the raw_material product.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module - Material Master (MARA)

description: Foreign key linking to compliance.eccn_classification. Business justification: Raw materials (chemicals, substrates, gases) require ECCN classification for international procurement, import/export documentation, and supplier compliance verification in semiconductor supply chain

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Raw material ownership by legal entity required for inventory valuation, tax reporting, intercompany material transfers, and legal entity P&L allocation.

Foreign key linking to supply.material_master. Business justification: Raw_material (physical inventory) must link to material_master (supply chain master data) to inherit procurement parameters, quality specifications, lead times, compliance attributes, and valuation ru

Foreign key linking to product.process_node. Business justification: Raw materials (wafers, substrates) are qualified for specific process nodes. Procurement and supplier qualification depend on process node compatibility for fab operations.
FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Raw materials need a default storage location for receiving and replenishment workflows.

Foreign key linking to compliance.substance_inventory. Business justification: Raw materials must link to substance inventory for REACH SVHC tracking, RoHS compliance verification, conflict minerals reporting, and hazardous material safety data sheet management required by EU an

Foreign key linking to supply.supplier. Business justification: PROCUREMENT: Supplier of each raw material is required for qualification, lead‑time, and compliance tracking; experts expect a direct supplier_id FK.

The primary unit of measure in which the raw material is stocked and consumed (e.g., EA for each wafer, KG for chemicals, L for liquids, M2 for photomasks). Corresponds to SAP MARA.MEINS (base unit of measure). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: EA|KG|L|M2|M3|PC|SHT|BOX|DRUM|CYLINDER — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.MEINS; ISO 80000; SEMI E10

description: Indicates whether the raw material is managed at the batch/lot level in SAP inventory management. Batch management is mandatory for silicon wafers, photomasks, and shelf-life-controlled chemicals to enable full lot traceability from receipt through consumption. Corresponds to SAP MARA.XCHPF.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.XCHPF; SEMI E142 (Lot Traceability); ISO 9001

description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where the raw material was manufactured or substantially transformed. Required for export control compliance (EAR/ITAR), customs declarations, and CHIPS Act domestic content tracking.
reference: ISO 3166-1 alpha-3; BIS/EAR (Export Administration Regulations); ITAR; CHIPS Act

description: Timestamp when the raw material master record was first created in the system of record (SAP S/4HANA MM). Used for audit trail, data lineage, and compliance with SOX internal controls over financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.ERSDA / MARA.ERNAM; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001

description: Date on which the raw material is scheduled to be discontinued or reaches end-of-life (EOL). Triggers last-time-buy (LTB) procurement actions and product change notification (PCN) processes. Null if the material is not scheduled for discontinuation.
reference: Oracle Agile PLM - PCN/EOL Management; SAP S/4HANA MM; JEDEC JESD48

description: Fixed order quantity used when lot_size_type is 'fixed'. Represents the standard purchase order quantity per replenishment cycle, often aligned with supplier minimum order quantities or packaging units. Corresponds to SAP MARC.BSTFE.

GHS/UN hazard classification of the raw material for safe storage, handling, and transport. Drives storage location assignment, PPE requirements, and emergency response procedures. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: non_hazardous|flammable|corrosive|toxic|oxidizer|pyrophoric|compressed_gas|carcinogen — promote to reference product]
reference: GHS (Globally Harmonized System of Classification and Labelling); OSHA 29 CFR 1910.1200; ISO 45001

description: Indicates whether incoming quality inspection (IQC) is mandatory upon goods receipt for this raw material. When true, received lots are placed in quality inspection stock in SAP QM before being released to unrestricted use. Corresponds to SAP MARC.QKZUL.
reference: SAP S/4HANA QM - MARC.QKZUL; ISO 9001 Clause 8.6; IATF 16949

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled
description: Indicates whether the raw material is subject to ITAR controls under the US Munitions List. ITAR-controlled materials require State Department licensing for export and impose strict access and handling restrictions.
reference: ITAR (22 CFR Parts 120-130); US Munitions List (USML)

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to the raw material master record. Supports change tracking, delta extraction for the Silver layer, and audit compliance. Corresponds to SAP MARA.LAEDA (last change date).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.LAEDA; SOX Section 404; ISO 9001

description: Planned procurement lead time in calendar days from purchase order placement to goods receipt. Used by SAP MRP for replenishment scheduling and safety stock calculations. Corresponds to SAP MARC.PLIFZ (planned delivery time).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP - MARC.PLIFZ; SEMI E10

description: SAP MRP lot-sizing procedure that determines how replenishment quantities are calculated. 'fixed' orders a fixed quantity, 'lot_for_lot' orders exact requirement, 'min_max' uses min/max boundaries, 'periodic' groups requirements by period. Corresponds to SAP MARC.DISMM.

Maximum allowable inventory quantity for the raw material, used to prevent overstocking of hazardous materials, shelf-life-limited chemicals, or high-cost items. Corresponds to SAP MARC.MABST (maximum stock level).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP - MARC.MABST; ISO 14001 (hazardous material quantity limits)

description: Minimum number of days of remaining shelf life required at the time of goods receipt for the material to be accepted into inventory. Materials received with less than this remaining shelf life are rejected or placed in restricted use. Corresponds to SAP MARA.MHDLP.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.MHDLP; ISO 9001; IATF 16949

description: Current moving average price per base unit of measure, recalculated with each goods receipt based on actual purchase prices. Used for inventory valuation under the moving average price control method. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VERPR.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - MBEW.VERPR; IFRS IAS 2; SOX Section 404

description: Determines the inventory valuation method for the material: 'standard' uses a fixed standard price, 'moving_average' recalculates price with each goods movement. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.VPRSV (price control indicator: S or V).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - MBEW.VPRSV; IFRS IAS 2; SOX

description: ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard price and moving average price fields (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY, TWD). Required for multi-currency financial reporting and SOX compliance.
reference: ISO 4217; SAP S/4HANA FI - MBEW.WAERS; SOX

description: Chemical purity of the raw material expressed as a percentage (e.g., 99.9999 for 6N-grade specialty gases, 99.99 for ultra-high-purity chemicals). Critical quality specification for process chemicals, specialty gases, and ALD precursors where trace contamination causes yield loss.
reference: SEMI C1 (Ultra-High Purity Chemicals); SEMI C7.4; ISO 9001

business_glossary_term: Registration Evaluation Authorization and Restriction of Chemicals (REACH) Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) Flag
description: Indicates whether the raw material contains or is classified as a Substance of Very High Concern (SVHC) under the EU REACH Regulation. Triggers mandatory disclosure obligations when SVHC concentration exceeds 0.1% w/w in articles.
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006 Article 57; ECHA SVHC Candidate List

description: Inventory level at which a replenishment purchase requisition is automatically triggered in SAP MRP. When on-hand stock falls to or below this quantity, procurement is initiated to prevent production line stoppages. Corresponds to SAP MARC.MINBE (reorder point).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP - MARC.MINBE; SEMI E10

description: Electrical resistivity of the silicon wafer substrate in Ohm·cm, indicating doping concentration and conductivity type. Critical specification for FEOL process compatibility and device electrical performance. Null for non-wafer materials.
reference: SEMI M1; SEMI M6 (Resistivity Measurement); JEDEC JESD22

description: Indicates whether the raw material complies with the EU RoHS Directive (2011/65/EU) restricting the use of hazardous substances including lead, mercury, cadmium, hexavalent chromium, PBB, and PBDE. Required for materials used in products shipped to EU markets.

Minimum buffer stock quantity maintained to protect against supply disruptions, demand variability, and supplier lead time uncertainty. Represents the floor below which inventory should not fall under normal operating conditions. Corresponds to SAP MARC.EISBE (safety stock).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM/MRP - MARC.EISBE; SEMI E10

description: Indicates whether individual units of the raw material are tracked by unique serial number. Applicable to high-value items such as photomasks and reticles where individual unit traceability is required for defect root cause analysis and reticle qualification tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Serial Number Management; SEMI P1 (Photomask); Oracle Agile PLM

description: Maximum number of days the raw material can be stored from the date of manufacture or receipt before it is considered expired and must be quarantined or disposed of. Critical for photoresists, ALD precursors, and specialty chemicals with time-sensitive stability. Corresponds to SAP MARA.MHDRZ (shelf life in days).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - MARA.MHDRZ; SEMI C1; ISO 9001

description: Standard cost price per base unit of measure used for inventory valuation in SAP CO (Controlling) and FI (Financial Accounting). Used for WIP costing, variance analysis, and financial reporting. Corresponds to SAP MBEW.STPRS (standard price).
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO - MBEW.STPRS; SOX Section 404; IFRS IAS 2 (Inventories)

description: Categorical storage environment required for the raw material. Drives warehouse location assignment and storage area classification. Examples: cleanroom for bare silicon wafers, refrigerated for certain photoresists, nitrogen_purge for moisture-sensitive ALD precursors. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ambient|refrigerated|frozen|cleanroom|nitrogen_purge|dark_storage|fireproof_cabinet — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
reference: SEMI C1; SEMI M1; GHS SDS Section 7; ISO 14001

description: Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage for storage of the raw material. Particularly critical for silicon wafers, photomasks, and hygroscopic chemicals where moisture exposure causes defects or degradation.
reference: SEMI M1 (Silicon Wafer Standard); SEMI P1 (Photomask Standard); GHS SDS Section 7

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the raw material. Exceeding this threshold may degrade material quality, trigger safety hazards, or invalidate process qualification. Used for warehouse environmental compliance monitoring.
reference: SEMI C1; GHS SDS Section 7; ISO 14001

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for the raw material. Used to configure environmental monitoring alerts in the warehouse management system and ensure compliance with material safety data sheet (SDS) requirements.
reference: SEMI C1; GHS SDS Section 7; ISO 14001

description: Nominal diameter of the silicon wafer in millimeters (e.g., 200.0 for 8-inch, 300.0 for 12-inch). Applicable only to silicon wafer material class. Critical for process tool compatibility and fab line qualification. Null for non-wafer materials.
reference: SEMI M1 (Specification for Polished Monocrystalline Silicon Wafers); SEMI M49

description: Specifies the silicon wafer substrate type: bare_silicon (polished CZ), epitaxial (epi-layer deposited), soi (Silicon-on-Insulator), fz (Float Zone), or cz (Czochralski). Determines process compatibility and electrical characteristics. Null for non-wafer materials.
reference: SEMI M1; SEMI M44 (SOI Wafers); JEDEC

description: Unique surrogate identifier for the storage location master record within the inventory management system. Primary key for the storage_location entity.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM Module — Storage Location (LGORT)

description: Physical access control level for this storage location. unrestricted — open access; badge-access — requires valid facility badge; authorized-personnel — restricted to named personnel list (e.g., for ITAR-controlled materials); security-vault — highest security for KGD die banks, photomask vaults, and ITAR-controlled IP. Supports ITAR/EAR export control compliance.
reference: ITAR — International Traffic in Arms Regulations; BIS/EAR — Export Administration Regulations; ISO 45001

description: Bay designation within the building or cleanroom where this storage location is physically situated (e.g., 'BAY-03', 'A-WEST'). Used for precise physical navigation, equipment layout planning, and cleanroom zone management per SEMI E187.
reference: SEMI E187 — Cleanroom Layout; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES

description: Identifier for the physical building within the facility where this storage location resides (e.g., 'FAB1', 'CHEM-WH-B', 'OSAT-PKG-C'). Supports facility layout mapping, emergency response planning, and environmental zone management.
reference: SEMI S2 — Facility Safety; ISO 14001 Environmental Management

description: Unit of measure used to express the maximum capacity and current utilization of this storage location. Varies by facility type: cassette (wafer storage), pod (photomask storage), tray (packaged chip storage), container (chemical storage), box (die bank storage), pallet (bulk warehouse storage).
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Unit of Measure (MEINS); JEDEC JESD22 — Package Handling

description: ISO 14644-1 cleanroom classification for this storage location, defining the maximum allowable airborne particulate concentration. Critical for wafer, photomask, and KGD die storage compliance. Applicable only to cleanroom facility types; null for non-cleanroom locations. Values: ISO-1 (cleanest) through ISO-6.
reference: ISO 14644-1 — Cleanroom Classification; SEMI E187 — Semiconductor Cleanroom Standards

description: Date on which this storage location was formally commissioned and made available for inventory use. Marks the start of the location's operational lifecycle. Used for asset age tracking, depreciation scheduling, and facility capacity planning.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Storage Location Master; ISO 9001 — Quality Management Records

description: Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the country where this storage location is physically situated (e.g., 'USA', 'TWN', 'KOR', 'JPN'). Critical for ITAR/EAR export control jurisdiction determination, REACH/RoHS regulatory applicability, and multi-national inventory reporting.
reference: ISO 3166-1 Alpha-3 — Country Codes; ITAR 22 CFR Part 120; BIS/EAR 15 CFR Part 734

description: Timestamp when this storage location master record was first created in the system (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Provides audit trail for record provenance and data governance compliance per SOX and ISO 9001 quality management requirements.
reference: SOX — Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404; ISO 9001 — Quality Management Records; GDPR Article 5 — Data Integrity

description: Current number of inventory units occupying this storage location at the time of last inventory update. Compared against max_capacity_units to compute utilization percentage for capacity planning and inventory placement decisions. Updated by MES and SAP MM inventory movements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Stock Overview; Camstar MES — Lot Location Tracking

description: Date on which this storage location was permanently decommissioned and removed from active inventory use. Populated only when location_status is 'decommissioned'. Supports facility lifecycle management and historical inventory traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Storage Location Master; ISO 9001 — Quality Management Records

description: ESD protection classification of this storage location per ANSI/ESD S20.20 and JEDEC JESD625 standards. Determines suitability for storing ESD-sensitive devices (ICs, dies, packaged chips). class-0 (most sensitive, <250V HBM) through class-3 (>2000V HBM); unprotected for non-ESD-controlled areas.
reference: ANSI/ESD S20.20 — ESD Control Program; JEDEC JESD625 — ESD Sensitive Device Handling

description: Classification of the physical facility type housing this storage location. Drives environmental compliance rules, MSD floor life management, and hazmat handling procedures. Valid values: cleanroom (ISO-classified FAB area), warehouse (general materials storage), cold-storage (temperature-controlled for chemicals/biologics), gas-cabinet (pressurized specialty gas storage), nitrogen-cabinet (nitrogen-purged dry storage for moisture-sensitive devices), die-vault (secure KGD die bank storage).
reference: SEMI S2 — Environmental, Health & Safety Guideline; SEMI E187 — Cleanroom Classification

description: Type of fire suppression system installed at this storage location. Critical for chemical warehouse and gas cabinet locations storing flammable or reactive materials. Drives emergency response planning and insurance compliance. Values: sprinkler, halon (legacy), fm200 (clean agent), co2, dry-powder, none.
reference: SEMI S2 — Environmental, Health & Safety; ISO 14001 — Environmental Management; NFPA 318 — Cleanroom Fire Protection

description: Geographic latitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the facility where this storage location resides. Used for multi-site supply chain mapping, emergency response planning, and regulatory jurisdiction determination for ITAR/EAR export control compliance.
reference: WGS84 — World Geodetic System; BIS/EAR — Geographic Jurisdiction

description: Geographic longitude coordinate (WGS84 decimal degrees) of the facility where this storage location resides. Used in conjunction with geo_latitude for multi-site supply chain mapping and regulatory jurisdiction determination.
reference: WGS84 — World Geodetic System; BIS/EAR — Geographic Jurisdiction

description: UN/DOT hazardous materials classification code applicable to materials stored at this location (e.g., 'UN1170' for ethanol, 'UN2014' for hydrogen peroxide, 'FLAMMABLE-LIQUID', 'CORROSIVE', 'COMPRESSED-GAS', 'NON-HAZMAT'). Governs storage segregation, emergency response, and regulatory reporting under REACH, RoHS, and TSCA. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: flammable-liquid|corrosive|compressed-gas|oxidizer|toxic|non-hazmat|pyrophoric — promote to reference product]
reference: REACH Regulation (EC) No 1907/2006; RoHS Directive 2011/65/EU; TSCA — Toxic Substances Control Act; UN GHS — Globally Harmonized System

description: Inventory valuation method applied to stock held at this storage location for financial reporting purposes. standard-cost (used for WIP wafer lots), moving-average (raw materials), fifo (finished goods), fefo (shelf-life-managed chemicals). Aligns with SAP S/4HANA FI/CO valuation area configuration and SOX financial reporting requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI/CO — Inventory Valuation; SOX — Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404; IAS 2 — Inventories

business_glossary_term: Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Partner Location Flag
description: Indicates whether this storage location is physically situated at an OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) partner facility rather than an internal company site. True for consignment stock locations at OSAT partners. Drives consignment inventory valuation, ownership tracking, and inter-company transfer accounting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Consignment Stock; JEDEC JESD31 — OSAT Inventory Management

business_glossary_term: International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR) Controlled Flag
description: Indicates whether this storage location is designated for ITAR-controlled materials, requiring strict access logging, personnel authorization, and regulatory reporting. True for locations storing defense-related ICs, ASIC designs, or photomasks subject to ITAR jurisdiction. Drives export control compliance workflows.
reference: ITAR — 22 CFR Parts 120-130; BIS/EAR — 15 CFR Parts 730-774

business_glossary_term: Known Good Die (KGD) Storage Certified Flag
description: Indicates whether this storage location is certified for KGD (Known Good Die) die bank storage, meeting requirements for ESD protection, nitrogen purge, temperature/humidity control, and access security. KGD storage certification is required for high-value tested dies awaiting packaging or shipment.
reference: JEDEC JESD31 — KGD Storage Requirements; SEMI G42 — Die Bank Management

description: Date of the most recent environmental and safety inspection conducted for this storage location. Used to track compliance with SEMI S2, ISO 14001, and ISO 45001 periodic inspection requirements. Drives inspection scheduling and regulatory audit readiness.
reference: SEMI S2 — Environmental, Health & Safety; ISO 14001 — Environmental Management; ISO 45001 — Occupational Health and Safety

description: Timestamp of the most recent modification to this storage location master record (ISO 8601 format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Used for change data capture (CDC), data synchronization between SAP S/4HANA and the Databricks Silver Layer, and audit trail maintenance.
reference: SOX — Sarbanes-Oxley Act Section 404; ISO 9001 — Quality Management Records; GDPR Article 5

description: Externally-known alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the storage location, aligned with SAP S/4HANA MM storage location key (LGORT) and Camstar MES location identifiers. Used for lot traceability, WIP tracking, and inventory placement across FAB, warehouse, and OSAT partner facilities.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Storage Location Master; Camstar MES Location Management

description: Human-readable descriptive name of the storage location (e.g., 'Bay 3 Shelf 12 — EUV Photomask Vault', 'Cleanroom A Chemical Cabinet Row 5'). Used in operational reports, MES screens, and inventory placement instructions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Storage Location Master; SEMI E10 Equipment Identification

description: Current operational lifecycle status of the storage location. 'active' — available for inventory placement; 'blocked' — temporarily unavailable due to maintenance, contamination, or audit hold; 'decommissioned' — permanently retired from use. Controls whether new inventory can be assigned to this location.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Storage Location Status; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES Location State

description: Maximum number of inventory units (wafer cassettes, chemical containers, photomask pods, chip trays, or die boxes) that can be physically stored at this location. Used for capacity planning, inventory placement optimization, and utilization reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Storage Bin Capacity; Applied Materials SmartFactory MES Capacity Management

description: Maximum allowable relative humidity percentage (0–100%) for this storage location. Exceedance above maximum triggers MSD floor life reset, inventory quarantine, and environmental non-conformance reporting per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — MSD Handling; SEMI E187 — Cleanroom Environmental Control

description: Maximum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Defines the upper bound of the acceptable temperature range. Exceedance triggers inventory quarantine and environmental non-conformance reporting per ISO 14001 and SEMI C10 chemical storage guidelines.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — MSD Handling; SEMI C10 — Chemical Storage; ISO 14001

description: Minimum allowable relative humidity percentage (0–100%) for this storage location. Critical for MSD floor life management, photomask storage, and chemical stability. Exceedance below minimum may cause ESD risk or material degradation.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — MSD Handling; SEMI E187 — Cleanroom Environmental Control

description: Minimum allowable storage temperature in degrees Celsius for this location. Defines the lower bound of the acceptable temperature range for stored materials (chemicals, photoresists, gases, packaged devices). Used for environmental compliance monitoring and MSD (Moisture Sensitive Device) floor life management per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — Moisture Sensitive Device Handling; SEMI C10 — Chemical Storage

business_glossary_term: Moisture Sensitive Device (MSD) Floor Life Management Capable Flag
description: Indicates whether this storage location is equipped and certified to manage MSD floor life tracking per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033. True for nitrogen-purged cabinets, dry-storage cabinets, and controlled-humidity cleanroom locations. Enables automatic floor life clock suspension for stored MSDs.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — Handling, Packing, Shipping and Use of Moisture/Reflow Sensitive SMDs

description: Maximum Moisture Sensitivity Level (MSL) of devices that can be safely stored at this location per IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020. MSL-1 (unlimited floor life at ≤30°C/85%RH) through MSL-5 (most sensitive, 24-hour floor life). Determines which packaged semiconductor devices are eligible for placement at this location.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-020 — Moisture/Reflow Sensitivity Classification; J-STD-033 — MSD Handling

description: Scheduled date for the next mandatory environmental and safety inspection of this storage location. Automatically calculated based on inspection frequency requirements per SEMI S2 and ISO 14001. Triggers inspection work orders in the facility management system.
reference: SEMI S2 — Environmental, Health & Safety; ISO 14001 — Environmental Management

description: Indicates whether this storage location is equipped with nitrogen purge capability to maintain an inert, moisture-free atmosphere. True for nitrogen-cabinet and die-vault locations used for long-term KGD die storage, photomask preservation, and moisture-sensitive chemical storage. Drives MSD floor life management decisions.
reference: IPC/JEDEC J-STD-033 — MSD Handling; SEMI C10 — Chemical Storage Guidelines

business_glossary_term: Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test (OSAT) Partner Name
description: Name of the OSAT partner company at whose facility this storage location resides (e.g., 'ASE Group', 'Amkor Technology', 'JCET'). Populated only when is_osat_partner_location is True. Used for consignment stock management, partner performance reporting, and supply chain visibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Vendor Master; Salesforce CRM — Partner Account

description: Indicates whether this storage location meets the environmental and cleanliness requirements for photomask (reticle) storage, including ISO cleanroom class, nitrogen purge, and ESD protection. Photomasks are high-value assets used in EUV/DUV lithography and require specialized storage conditions.
reference: SEMI P1 — Specification for Photomask Handling; SEMI E187 — Cleanroom Standards

description: SAP S/4HANA plant identifier to which this storage location belongs. Represents the manufacturing or distribution facility (FAB, OSAT partner site, or warehouse) in the enterprise organizational hierarchy. Enables multi-plant inventory management and inter-plant transfer tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Plant (WERKS); SEMI E10 Facility Identification

description: Row designation within the bay for this storage location (e.g., 'R-12', 'ROW-A'). Provides granular physical addressing for inventory placement, pick-and-put-away operations, and automated storage/retrieval system (ASRS) integration.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Warehouse Row; Camstar MES Location Hierarchy

description: Shelf or bin designation within the row for this storage location (e.g., 'S-04', 'BIN-002'). Lowest level of the physical location hierarchy, enabling precise inventory placement and retrieval for wafer cassettes, chemical containers, photomasks, and packaged chip trays.
reference: SAP S/4HANA WM — Storage Bin; Camstar MES Bin Management

description: Indicates whether shelf life expiry tracking is enabled for materials stored at this location. True for chemical storage, photoresist storage, and gas cabinet locations where materials have defined shelf lives. Enables automated expiry alerts and FEFO (First Expired First Out) inventory management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM — Shelf Life Management (MHDRZ); SEMI C10 — Chemical Shelf Life

description: Maximum allowable total weight in kilograms for materials stored at this location. Critical for shelf structural integrity compliance, particularly for heavy chemical drums, gas cylinders, and bulk wafer cassette storage. Enforced per facility safety standards.
reference: SEMI S2 — Environmental, Health & Safety Guideline; ISO 45001 — Occupational Health and Safety

business_glossary_term: Work in Process (WIP) Staging Area Flag
description: Indicates whether this storage location is designated as a WIP (Work in Process) staging area for wafer lots between process steps. True for inter-bay buffers, equipment load ports, and process queue areas within the FAB cleanroom. Enables WIP flow tracking and cycle time analysis in the MES.
reference: Applied Materials SmartFactory MES — WIP Management; Camstar MES — Lot Tracking; SEMI E10 — Equipment Productivity

description: Unique identifier for the goods movement transaction. Primary key for the goods movement record.
Identifier of the customer for goods issue transactions related to sales orders or consignment stock transfers. Links to customer master for finished goods shipments.
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost center on each goods movement is needed for accurate cost allocation in financial statements.

Foreign key linking to compliance.export_license_usage. Business justification: Each goods movement (shipment) must record the specific export license usage to satisfy license utilization reporting and audit trails.

Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: Goods movements record shipments and receipts of finished goods (packaged chips) to/from customers, warehouses, and OSAT partners. Adding finished_good_id FK enables goods movement records to referenc
Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: GL account on movement ties inventory transactions to the general ledger for audit‑ready posting.

Foreign key linking to supply.goods_receipt. Business justification: Goods movements receiving material from suppliers must reference the originating goods_receipt to maintain full ERP traceability from PO→GR→goods movement→stock balance. Standard material flow in semi

Foreign key linking to inventory.inventory_wafer_lot. Business justification: Goods movements record every inventory movement event including WIP wafer lot transfers between work centers, storage locations, and process stages. Adding inventory_wafer_lot_id FK enables goods move

Foreign key linking to finance.journal_entry_line. Business justification: Goods movements generate journal entry lines for inventory accounting postings - direct operational link for goods receipt, issue, and transfer transactions.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Goods movements must track legal entity for intercompany billing, customs documentation, financial posting to correct legal entity books, and audit trail.
Foreign key linking to order.order. Business justification: REQUIRED: Financial and logistics reporting ties inventory movements to the originating sales order for cost of goods sold and compliance.
Identifier of the material being moved. References the material master record for silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks, packaged chips, or die banks.

FK to inventory.stock_balance.stock_balance_id — Every goods movement posting updates the stock balance. This is the fundamental inventory equation: stock_balance = sum(goods_movements). Critical for reconciliation and audit.
FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Every goods movement must reference source location for audit trail and inventory reconciliation.

Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order. Business justification: LOGISTICS: Each goods movement (receipt/shipment) is driven by a PO; linking supports audit, costing, and on‑time delivery metrics.

Foreign key linking to inventory.raw_material. Business justification: Goods movements record goods receipts from suppliers for raw materials (silicon wafers, chemicals, gases, photomasks) and goods issues to production. Adding raw_material_id FK enables goods movement r

Foreign key linking to compliance.restricted_party_screening. Business justification: Shipments require restricted party screening results for export clearance, denied party list verification, and customs compliance. Essential for preventing unauthorized exports and sanctions violation

Foreign key linking to product.sku. Business justification: Goods movements (receipts, issues, transfers) are tracked by SKU for lot genealogy, inventory costing, and traceability. Required for GAAP/IFRS inventory accounting.

FK to inventory.storage_location.storage_location_id — Every goods movement has a source location (for issues, transfers) or destination location (for receipts). Location reference is mandatory for movement posting and audit trail.

Identifier of the supplier for goods receipt transactions. Links to vendor master for silicon wafer suppliers, chemical vendors, gas suppliers, or OSAT (Outsourced Semiconductor Assembly and Test) partners.
Foreign key linking to finance.wbs_element. Business justification: Goods movements for project-specific materials charge to WBS elements for project cost tracking and customer billing support.
Batch or lot number for batch-managed materials such as chemicals, gases, or raw wafers. Distinct from wafer lot number; used for supplier batch traceability and shelf-life management.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Batch Management, REACH Regulation - Chemical Traceability

description: Quality bin or grade classification for die or wafer inventory: Bin 1 (prime), Bin 2 (downgrade), Bin 3 (scrap), or custom bin codes. Used for Known Good Die (KGD) status tracking and yield analysis.
reference: SEMI E142 - Substrate Tracking, JEDEC Standards - Die Quality Classification

description: Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the valuation amount. Typically company currency (USD, EUR, JPY, etc.) for inventory accounting.
Delivery note or packing slip number from the supplier or shipping carrier. Used for goods receipt matching and three-way match (PO, goods receipt, invoice) reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Receipt, SOX Section 404

description: Plant or FAB site code where the material was moved to. Used for inter-plant transfers and multi-site inventory tracking. Critical for global supply chain visibility.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant Master, SEMI E120 - Lot Traceability

description: Storage location code where the material was moved to. Represents warehouse, stockroom, or FAB storage area. Empty for goods issues to external customers or scrap.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Storage Location Master

description: Line item number within the material document. Supports multi-line goods movement documents where multiple materials or lots are moved in a single transaction.
Unique material document number generated by the ERP system for this goods movement. Primary business identifier for the transaction, used for audit trail and reversal references.
Wafer lot or batch identifier for Work in Process (WIP) inventory. Critical for lot traceability and IATF 16949 compliance. Tracks wafer lots through fabrication, die banks, and packaging.
reference: IATF 16949 - Automotive Quality Management, SEMI E120 - Lot Traceability

description: Date when the physical goods movement occurred. Business event date for inventory transaction, distinct from posting date. Critical for SOX Section 404 audit trail and inventory valuation.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls, SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Movement

description: Classification of the inventory movement transaction: goods receipt from supplier, goods issue to production, inter-location transfer, scrap disposal, return from production, or posting reversal. Aligns with SAP MM movement type codes.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Movement Types

description: Free-text notes or comments about the goods movement transaction. Used for documenting special handling instructions, quality issues, or operational exceptions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Document Text

description: Date when the goods movement was posted to the financial ledger. Used for period-end closing and financial reporting. May differ from movement date for backdated or delayed postings.
reference: SAP S/4HANA FI - Financial Accounting, SOX Section 404

description: Production order number for goods issues to manufacturing or goods receipts from production. Links inventory movements to wafer fabrication, die packaging, or assembly operations.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Production Order, SEMI E120 - Lot Traceability

description: Quantity of material moved in this transaction. Positive for receipts and transfers-in, negative for issues and transfers-out. Precision supports wafer counts, die counts, and chemical volumes.

Code indicating the business reason for the goods movement: quality hold, scrap due to defect, return to vendor, obsolescence, cycle count adjustment, or other operational reason. Required for scrap and adjustment transactions.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Reason Codes, IATF 16949 - Nonconforming Product Control

description: Line item number within the reference document. Provides precise linkage to the specific line of the purchase order, production order, or delivery note.
Number of the source document that triggered this goods movement. Links to purchase order, production order, delivery note, or other originating transaction for full traceability.

Type of source document that triggered this goods movement: purchase order (PO) for receipts, production order for manufacturing issues/receipts, delivery note for shipments, transfer order for inter-location moves, sales order for customer shipments, or reservation for planned consumption.
Material reservation number for planned goods issues. Links to production order or maintenance order reservations for material requirements planning (MRP) and allocation.
Flag indicating whether this goods movement is a reversal of a previous transaction. True for cancellation or correction postings. Used for audit trail and error correction tracking.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Movement Reversal, SOX Section 404

description: Material document number of the original transaction being reversed. Populated only when reversal_indicator is true. Provides linkage for audit trail and reconciliation.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Goods Movement Reversal, SOX Section 404

description: Serial number for serialized inventory items such as high-value equipment parts or Known Good Die (KGD) units. Enables unit-level traceability for critical components.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Serial Number Management, IATF 16949 - Product Identification

description: Expiration date for time-sensitive materials such as photoresists, chemicals, or adhesives. Used for shelf-life management and FIFO (First In First Out) inventory control.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Shelf Life Management, REACH Regulation - Chemical Safety

description: Plant or FAB site code where the material originated. Used for inter-plant transfers and multi-site inventory tracking. Aligns with SEMI E120 traceability requirements.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Plant Master, SEMI E120 - Lot Traceability

description: Storage location code where the material was moved from. Represents warehouse, stockroom, or FAB storage area. Empty for goods receipts from external suppliers.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Storage Location Master

description: Indicator for special stock types: consignment stock at OSAT partners, project-specific stock, sales order allocated stock, returnable packaging, or pipeline inventory. Affects ownership and valuation rules.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Special Stock Management

description: Stock status classification: unrestricted (available for use), quality inspection (pending QA release), blocked (quality hold or obsolete), or restricted use (conditional release). Determines material availability for production and sales.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Stock Types, IATF 16949 - Control of Nonconforming Product

description: Precise date and time when the goods movement transaction was recorded in the system. Used for audit trail, sequence verification, and real-time inventory updates.
reference: SOX Section 404 - Internal Controls, ISO 8601 - Date and Time Format

description: Unit of measure for the movement quantity: wafers (EA), dies (PC), liters (L), kilograms (KG), or other standard units. Aligns with ISO 80000 quantity standards.
reference: ISO 80000 - Quantities and Units, SAP S/4HANA MM - Units of Measure

description: Total monetary value of the goods movement in company currency. Calculated as quantity multiplied by material standard cost or moving average price. Used for inventory valuation and financial reporting.
reference: SAP S/4HANA MM - Material Valuation, SOX Section 404

description: Work center or production resource where the material was issued or received. Identifies specific FAB equipment, packaging line, or assembly station for process traceability.
reference: SAP S/4HANA PP - Work Center Master, SEMI E120 - Equipment Traceability

description: Unique identifier for the inventory lot hold record. Primary key for the inventory lot hold transaction.
Identifier of the customer associated with the lot under hold. Applicable for customer-specific holds or customer-requested holds.
Identifier of the corrective action plan or CAPA (Corrective and Preventive Action) record associated with this hold. Links the hold to the formal corrective action process.

Foreign key linking to product.compliance_cert. Business justification: Holds may be placed for missing/expired compliance certifications. Cert-driven hold tracking for release authorization and trade compliance gate management requires this link.

Foreign key linking to inventory.die_bank. Business justification: Die bank inventory (KGD dies) can be placed on quality hold pending re-inspection, yield analysis, or compliance review. Adding die_bank_id FK enables hold records to reference the specific die bank u

Foreign key linking to product.errata. Business justification: Holds may be placed due to errata discovery. Errata-driven hold tracking for disposition decisions, customer approval, and workaround validation requires this link.
Identifier of the manufacturing equipment or tool associated with the hold. Links the hold to the specific machine or tool that processed the lot before the hold was placed.

Foreign key linking to product.family. Business justification: Holds may be placed at family level for systemic issues (process excursions, design errata). Family-level hold tracking for risk management and customer notification.

Foreign key linking to inventory.finished_good. Business justification: The inventory_lot_hold product description explicitly states it captures hold events placed on wafer lots OR finished goods inventory. The existing inventory_wafer_lot_id FK covers wafer lot holds, bu

business_glossary_term: Failure Mode and Effects Analysis (FMEA) Reference ID
description: Reference to the FMEA document or record associated with this hold. Links the hold to the failure mode analysis that identified the risk or defect mode.

Foreign key linking to design.ic_design_project. Business justification: Lot‑hold decisions are often triggered by design‑specific issues; linking hold records to the project enables root‑cause analysis.
Identifier of the wafer lot or finished goods lot subject to this hold. References the inventory lot under hold restriction.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Lot holds may trigger financial reserves or liability recognition at legal entity level for quality issues, requiring legal entity attribution.

Foreign key linking to product.product_ltb_notification. Business justification: Holds may be placed due to LTB status (pending customer approval for last-time-buy). LTB-driven hold tracking for release authorization and customer acceptance requires this link.

Foreign key linking to quality.nonconformance_report. Business justification: Nonconformance reports trigger inventory lot holds for containment. MRB workflows require linking holds to NCRs for disposition tracking, hold release authorization, and compliance with ISO 9001 and I

Foreign key linking to product.pcn. Business justification: Holds may be placed due to PCN-related issues (pending customer approval, qualification). PCN-driven hold tracking for release management and change control requires this link.
Identifier of the manufacturing process step where the hold was triggered. References the specific operation or stage in the fabrication flow (e.g., lithography, etch, CMP).

Foreign key linking to product.product_qualification_program. Business justification: Holds may be placed pending qualification program completion. Qual-driven hold tracking for release authorization and certification gate management requires this link.
Identifier of the product or device type associated with the lot under hold. References the IC design, SoC, or ASIC product code.

Foreign key linking to supply.supplier_corrective_action. Business justification: When supplier quality issues (incoming defects, contamination) trigger inventory lot holds, the hold must reference the SCAR to track containment actions, root cause analysis, and release criteria. Co

Foreign key linking to compliance.trade_compliance_hold. Business justification: Lot holds often stem from trade compliance issues; linking hold records to the underlying trade compliance hold provides traceability for release decisions.
Description of the immediate containment action taken to prevent further impact. Details the steps taken to isolate the affected lot and prevent similar issues in other lots.
Date and time when this hold record was first created in the system. Audit trail timestamp for record creation.
Number of defects detected that triggered the hold. Represents the count of non-conformances or defects identified during inspection or testing.
Total number of die (individual chips) in the lot subject to this hold. Applicable for finished goods or die bank inventory holds.
Calculated defect rate in parts per million for the lot under hold. Quality metric indicating the severity of the defect condition.

Export control classification code for the lot under hold. Indicates the regulatory classification under EAR (Export Administration Regulations) or ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations). Used for export-control holds.
reference: EAR (Export Administration Regulations), ITAR (International Traffic in Arms Regulations)

description: Code identifying the fabrication facility or site where the lot was processed. Indicates the manufacturing location associated with the hold.
Additional free-text comments or notes related to the hold. Provides supplementary information, investigation findings, or disposition rationale.
Date and time when the hold was placed on the lot. Represents the principal business event timestamp when the lot became restricted from further processing or shipment.

Final disposition decision for the lot after hold investigation. Indicates the action taken: rework (reprocess the lot), scrap (discard the lot), release (approve for continued flow), return (send back to supplier or customer), quarantine (extended hold pending further review), or retest (re-inspect or re-qualify).
Date and time when the hold automatically expires if not explicitly released. Null if no expiration is set. Used for time-limited holds that auto-release after a defined period.
Full name of the person or system that initiated the hold. Provides human-readable identification of the hold originator.
Business identifier for the hold event. Externally-known unique code assigned to this hold transaction for tracking and reference purposes.

Priority level assigned to the hold. Indicates the urgency and business impact of the hold (critical for safety or regulatory issues, high for customer impact, medium for process improvement, low for administrative holds).
Standardized code representing the specific reason for the hold. Maps to internal quality management or MES reason code taxonomy (e.g., DEFECT_EXCURSION, SPC_VIOLATION, CUSTOMER_RETURN, ITAR_REVIEW).
Detailed free-text explanation of the hold reason. Provides context and specifics beyond the hold reason code, including root cause details, defect description, or customer complaint reference.
Full name of the person or system that released the hold. Provides human-readable identification of the release authority.
Date and time when the hold was released or lifted. Null if the hold is still active. Marks the point when the lot was cleared for continued processing or shipment.

Current lifecycle state of the hold. Indicates whether the hold is currently active (lot is restricted), released (hold lifted and lot available), expired (hold auto-released after time limit), or cancelled (hold invalidated).

Classification of the hold event. Indicates the functional area or business reason category that triggered the hold (engineering investigation, quality issue, customer request, export control compliance, process deviation, equipment failure, or material defect). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: engineering|quality|customer|export_control|process|equipment|material — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Flag indicating whether the hold is mandated by regulatory compliance requirements (e.g., export control, environmental, safety). True if the hold is regulatory-driven, false otherwise.
Date and time when this hold record was last updated. Audit trail timestamp for record modification.

Standardized code representing the identified root cause of the issue that triggered the hold. Maps to internal root cause taxonomy (e.g., MATERIAL_DEFECT, PROCESS_DRIFT, EQUIPMENT_FAILURE, OPERATOR_ERROR).
Number of wafers in the lot subject to this hold. Represents the count of silicon wafers affected by the hold restriction.
Unique identifier for the physical inventory count event record.

Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Physical inventory counts must be attributed to legal entity for audit trail, financial reconciliation, and legal entity-level inventory accuracy reporting.
